title: Okazaki Fragment
summary: A short stretch of newly synthesized DNA on the structures/lagging-strand. Each fragment is started by an RNA primer laid down by enzymes/rna-primase and extended 5' → 3' by enzymes/dna-polymerase-iii until it reaches the previous fragment's primer. Then enzymes/dna-polymerase-i replaces the RNA primer with DNA, and enzymes/dna-ligase seals the gap. Eukaryotic Okazaki fragments are ~100–200 nt; pr
